speaker: MORTIMER
text: Kind keepers of my weak decaying age,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Let dying Mortimer here rest himself.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Even like a man new-halèd from the rack,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: So fare my limbs with long imprisonment;

speaker: MORTIMER
text: And these gray locks, the pursuivants of death,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Nestor-like agèd in an age of care,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Argue the end of Edmund Mortimer;

speaker: MORTIMER
text: These eyes, like lamps whose wasting oil is spent,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Wax dim, as drawing to their exigent;

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Weak shoulders, overborne with burdening grief,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: And pithless arms, like to a withered vine

speaker: MORTIMER
text: That droops his sapless branches to the ground;

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Yet are these feet, whose strengthless stay is numb,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Unable to support this lump of clay,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Swift-wingèd with desire to get a grave,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: As witting I no other comfort have.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: But tell me, keeper, will my nephew come?

speaker: KEEPER
text: Richard Plantagenet, my lord, will come.

speaker: KEEPER
text: We sent unto the Temple, unto his chamber,

speaker: KEEPER
text: And answer was returned that he will come.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Enough. My soul shall then be satisfied.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Poor gentleman, his wrong doth equal mine.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Since Henry Monmouth first began to reign,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Before whose glory I was great in arms,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: This loathsome sequestration have I had;

speaker: MORTIMER
text: And even since then hath Richard been obscured,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Deprived of honor and inheritance.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: But now the arbitrator of despairs,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Just Death, kind umpire of men’s miseries,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: With sweet enlargement doth dismiss me hence.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: I would his troubles likewise were expired,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: That so he might recover what was lost.

speaker: KEEPER
text: My lord, your loving nephew now is come.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Richard Plantagenet, my friend, is he come?

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Ay, noble uncle, thus ignobly used,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Your nephew, late despisèd Richard, comes.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Direct mine arms I may embrace his neck

speaker: MORTIMER
text: And in his bosom spend my latter gasp.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: O, tell me when my lips do touch his cheeks,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: That I may kindly give one fainting kiss.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: And now declare, sweet stem from York’s great stock,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Why didst thou say of late thou wert despised?

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: First, lean thine agèd back against mine arm,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: And in that ease I’ll tell thee my disease.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: This day, in argument upon a case,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Some words there grew ’twixt Somerset and me,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Among which terms he used his lavish tongue

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: And did upbraid me with my father’s death;

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Which obloquy set bars before my tongue,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Else with the like I had requited him.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Therefore, good uncle, for my father’s sake,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: In honor of a true Plantagenet,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: And for alliance’ sake, declare the cause

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: My father, Earl of Cambridge, lost his head.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: That cause, fair nephew, that imprisoned me

speaker: MORTIMER
text: And hath detained me all my flow’ring youth

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Within a loathsome dungeon, there to pine,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Was cursèd instrument of his decease.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Discover more at large what cause that was,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: For I am ignorant and cannot guess.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: I will, if that my fading breath permit

speaker: MORTIMER
text: And death approach not ere my tale be done.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Henry the Fourth, grandfather to this king,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Deposed his nephew Richard, Edward’s son,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: The first begotten and the lawful heir

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Of Edward king, the third of that descent;

speaker: MORTIMER
text: During whose reign the Percies of the north,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Finding his usurpation most unjust,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Endeavored my advancement to the throne.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: The reason moved these warlike lords to this

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Was, for that—young Richard thus removed,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Leaving no heir begotten of his body—

speaker: MORTIMER
text: I was the next by birth and parentage;

speaker: MORTIMER
text: For by my mother I derivèd am

speaker: MORTIMER
text: From Lionel, Duke of Clarence, third son

speaker: MORTIMER
text: To King Edward the Third; whereas he

speaker: MORTIMER
text: From John of Gaunt doth bring his pedigree,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Being but fourth of that heroic line.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: But mark: as in this haughty great attempt

speaker: MORTIMER
text: They laborèd to plant the rightful heir,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: I lost my liberty and they their lives.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Long after this, when Henry the Fifth,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Succeeding his father Bolingbroke, did reign,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Thy father, Earl of Cambridge then, derived

speaker: MORTIMER
text: From famous Edmund Langley, Duke of York,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Marrying my sister that thy mother was,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Again, in pity of my hard distress,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Levied an army, weening to redeem

speaker: MORTIMER
text: And have installed me in the diadem.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: But, as the rest, so fell that noble earl

speaker: MORTIMER
text: And was beheaded. Thus the Mortimers,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: In whom the title rested, were suppressed.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Of which, my lord, your Honor is the last.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: True, and thou seest that I no issue have

speaker: MORTIMER
text: And that my fainting words do warrant death.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Thou art my heir; the rest I wish thee gather.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: But yet be wary in thy studious care.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Thy grave admonishments prevail with me.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: But yet methinks my father’s execution

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Was nothing less than bloody tyranny.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: With silence, nephew, be thou politic;

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Strong-fixèd is the house of Lancaster,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: And, like a mountain, not to be removed.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: But now thy uncle is removing hence,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: As princes do their courts when they are cloyed

speaker: MORTIMER
text: With long continuance in a settled place.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: O uncle, would some part of my young years

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Might but redeem the passage of your age.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Thou dost then wrong me, as that slaughterer doth

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Which giveth many wounds when one will kill.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Mourn not, except thou sorrow for my good;

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Only give order for my funeral.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: And so farewell, and fair be all thy hopes,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: And prosperous be thy life in peace and war.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: And peace, no war, befall thy parting soul.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: In prison hast thou spent a pilgrimage,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: And like a hermit overpassed thy days.—

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Well, I will lock his counsel in my breast,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: And what I do imagine, let that rest.—

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Keepers, convey him hence, and I myself

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Will see his burial better than his life.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Here dies the dusky torch of Mortimer,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Choked with ambition of the meaner sort.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: And for those wrongs, those bitter injuries,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Which Somerset hath offered to my house,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: I doubt not but with honor to redress.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: And therefore haste I to the Parliament,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Either to be restorèd to my blood,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Or make mine ill th’ advantage of my good.
